title: United States attacks 140 targets in Iran and Iranians declare blockade of Strait of Hormuz
summary: Military tension escalates with mutual bombings and threats to close the strategic maritime passage.

title: Republican Senator Lindsey Graham, Trump ally, dies at 71
summary: The veteran politician, known for his hardline stance against Iran, died suddenly according to an official statement.

title: Independent candidate Hitoshi Urano wins Tagawa mayoral election after previous mayor's harassment scandal
summary: The former mayor, who resigned over sexual harassment, failed in his re-election bid against the new candidate.

title: World Cup semifinals set: France-Spain and England-Argentina
summary: Argentina and England won their quarter-final matches in extra time to join France and Spain in the next round.

title: Former Emir of Qatar Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani dies at 74
summary: The former monarch, founder of Al Jazeera and architect of Qatar's 2022 World Cup bid, died after an 18-year reign.

title: ASEAN resumes dialogue with Myanmar by accepting presence of its Foreign Minister at meeting
summary: For the first time since the coup, the regional bloc allows the military regime to participate in its talks.

title: Japan rallies against Poland and qualifies for final phase of women's volleyball Nations League
summary: The Japanese team achieved a comeback victory that secures a spot among the top eight of the tournament.

title: Yokozuna Takerufuji defeated on first day of Nagoya tournament
summary: The champion, returning after a break, was beaten by sekiwake Yoshinofuji, while the other yokozuna Hoshoryu won his match.

title: Former lawmaker Yoshihiro Matsuzaka wins Higashimatsuyama mayoral election
summary: The new mayor, an independent, won the election with a voter turnout of 44.51%.

title: Japan dominates wheelchair Wimbledon: Kamiji wins double and Oda retains title
summary: Yui Kamiji won the women's singles and doubles events, while Tokito Oda won his second consecutive Wimbledon.

title: Zelensky proposes a change of prime minister to strengthen his government
summary: The Ukrainian president seeks to renew the cabinet to maintain political cohesion during the war with Russia.

title: Jockey Yutaka Take reaches 5,000 career wins
summary: The legendary Japanese jockey achieved the milestone in his 40th season and promises to keep raising the record.

title: 30 years since E. coli outbreak in Sakai that sickened over 9,000 people
summary: The city remembers the tragic food poisoning incident that caused four deaths and led to changes in food safety.

title: Former Fire Agency director dies after suffering cardiac arrest on Mount Iwate
summary: The 70-year-old former senior official died while climbing with a friend.

title: Young woman missing after jet ski capsizes off coast of Chiba
summary: Rescue teams search for the woman, in her 20s, who was swept away by the current at the river mouth.

title: Akita Shinkansen suspends service between Morioka and Akita due to heavy rain
summary: Heavy rainfall forced the cancellation of all trains on the section, with resumption expected the following day.

title: Man arrested for murdering nurse with hammer blows in Sapporo
summary: The suspect, 33, turned himself in to police and confessed to the crime that occurred at the victim's home.

title: Father of student suffocated on a tatami mat demands justice 33 years later
summary: Three former students involved refuse to pay the compensation ordered for the boy's death in 1992.

title: Rainy season ends in Shikoku, five days earlier than usual
summary: The end of the rainy season brings extreme temperatures, with forecasts of up to 38 degrees Celsius in some areas.

title: Japan commemorates 10 years of South China Sea arbitration while Beijing criticizes its minister
summary: China launched strong criticism against Japanese Foreign Minister Takeshi Iwaya for his statements on the arbitral ruling.
